title: Jayson Tatum set to return vs. Wizards after injury management off-day
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jayson Tatum returns to play against the Washington Wizards after resting for Achilles injury management.
Celtics' injury report lists Baylor Scheierman and Derrick White as probable; Nikola Vucevic remains out.
Tatum played 27 minutes in each of his three games since returning from a right ruptured Achilles.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'War Machine' starring Alan Ritchson is now streaming on Netflix, featuring a soldier excelling at Army Ranger training.
'Zootopia 2' available on Disney+ features Nick and Judy encountering a mystery linked to the origins of the animal city.
'Scarpetta' starring Nicole Kidman is streaming on Prime Video, following a medical examiner revisiting past connections in a murder case.

title: 5 things to know about Patriots offensive lineman Alijah Vera-Tucker
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Patriots signed former Jets guard Alijah Vera-Tucker to strengthen their offensive line after a season with 68 sacks allowed.
Vera-Tucker, 26, has played 43 games in the NFL but missed entire seasons due to Achilles and triceps injuries.
Morgan Moses, a former teammate, influenced Vera-Tucker's decision to join the Patriots, along with the team's winning culture.

title: Alex Cora identified 2 players he views as leaders of the 2026 Red Sox
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Manager Alex Cora identifies Trevor Story and Garrett Crochet as the 2026 Red Sox team leaders.
Trevor Story led the 2025 Red Sox in home runs, RBI, and total hits, embodying a lead-by-example role.
Garrett Crochet, known for consistency and dominance, was MLB's strikeout leader with 255 in 2025.

title: 4 things to watch during Boston Legacy FC’s NWSL debut against Gotham FC
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Boston Legacy FC will make its NWSL debut against Gotham FC at Gillette Stadium on March 14, 2026.
Head coach Filipa Patão aims for a possession-style game; 14 of 24 players have never played in NWSL.
Gotham FC, defending NWSL champions, features stars like Rose Lavelle and Esther González, presenting a tough matchup.

title: Should Massachusetts stop changing its clocks?
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts lawmakers, led by Senate President Karen E. Spilka, are debating ending the state's twice-a-year clock change.
Senator John F. Keenan filed a bill (S.2157) proposing the adoption of Atlantic Standard Time year-round.
Research links clock changes to health issues like heart attacks, strokes, and public health disruptions.

title: Boston Pride For The People announces 2026 parade and festival date
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Boston Pride For The People announced the 2026 parade and festival for Saturday, June 6.
The parade starts at 11 a.m. in Copley Square and concludes at Boston Common with a festival.
Mayor Michelle Wu praised the event's inclusivity and highlighted its regional and national appeal.

title: Feds release teen detained by ICE in Marlborough
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: A 14-year-old Brazilian girl detained by ICE in Marlborough was released after a federal judge's order.
DHS claims the girl was rescued from alleged gang members; detained when no guardians identified.
The girl received a notice to appear in immigration court as efforts for her legal residency continue.

title: With ‘top guys’ struggling, a lineup reshuffle might be needed for Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins are experiencing a scoring slump, affecting their playoff positioning, as they're barely holding the final wild-card spot.
David Pastrnak, while successful on the power play, struggles at 5-on-5, prompting coach Marco Sturm to consider lineup changes.
Potential line adjustments include pairing Pastrnak with younger players Fraser Minten and Marat Khusnutdinov to boost scoring.

title: Swiss student came to Boston to study rehab. Now he’s running for trauma survivors.
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Alexis Cogne, a 26-year-old from Geneva, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for the Stepping Strong Center.
Cogne is a visiting researcher at Harvard Medical School, specializing in neurorehabilitation and supporting trauma survivors.
He aims to raise $10,000 for trauma research and rehabilitation at Brigham and Women's Hospital.
